name: Sarah Chen
summary: Staff-level platform engineer with 6+ years of experience designing and operating distributed systems that process millions of events at scale. Deep expertise in Python, Go, Kafka, Kubernetes, and AWS, with a track record of reducing infrastructure costs, improving system reliability, and mentoring engineering teams.

experience.title: Staff Software Engineer
bullets: Architected real-time event processing platform handling 2M+ events/hour using Kafka and Python, ensuring sub-second end-to-end latency for critical data pipelines
Led migration from monolithic architecture to distributed microservices, reducing deployment cycles from weekly to daily and improving system reliability
Designed multi-tenant data isolation layer supporting 500+ enterprise customers with zero cross-tenant data leakage
Established engineering standards and conducted architecture reviews for a team of 12, driving adoption of observability best practices across the organization
Reduced infrastructure costs by 35% through Kubernetes container optimization, autoscaling improvements, and resource right-sizing

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer
bullets: Built and operated distributed task scheduling system processing 50K+ jobs daily with 99.9% reliability across AWS infrastructure
Optimized PostgreSQL and Redis query patterns, reducing average API latency by [X%] and improving p95 response times
Introduced automated integration testing framework, reducing production rollbacks by 70% and accelerating release velocity
Mentored 4 junior developers through structured onboarding, pair programming, and weekly architecture discussions

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Developed high-throughput RESTful APIs serving 100K+ daily active users using Django and PostgreSQL
Built automated CI/CD pipeline with Jenkins, reducing release cycle from 2 weeks to 2 days
Implemented feature flagging system enabling safe rollouts for 15+ concurrent experiments across distributed services
